results.check: skill-creator quick_validate
Coordinator CLI and bundled remote validator Python syntax compilation
Bundled validator preserves assignment-snapshot and authorized-baseline Git validation
Task preview includes the selected adaptive-grill configuration, registered human owner, and additional outputs
Task contract hash protects the human collaboration configuration
Task contract hash independently protects the mandatory submission-confirmation policy
Adaptive Grill with an incompatible independence mode is rejected
human_collaboration none disables Grill but still requires human-submission-confirmation.yaml
Registered human owner confirmation binds the current main-output hash and personal stance before submit
Stale, missing, or mismatched confirmation cannot validate as a current member submission
Submission confirmation has gate_effect none and does not satisfy G1-G3 approval
Pre-1.7.5 assignments remain explicit legacy-not-required records without retroactive confirmation
Bundled validators preserve Member V1.8.0 history and enforce Member V1.8.1 assignment-acceptance semantics
Completed adaptive member submission is validated from the exact remote branch commit and projected as valid
Standalone coordinator package continues to install private README/SVG dashboard automation
Dashboard renderer produces valid native SVG for real, empty, unknown-stage, pending-merge, long-label, and more-than-eight-task states
Dashboard dynamic height keeps tasks and footer inside the viewBox
Dashboard suppresses local Git paths and uses current-stage statistics instead of global totals
Coordinator V1.7.6 assignments require ai-sop-member V1.7.5 or a compatible higher version
Visual QA against the user-provided dashboard reference
GitHub Issue notification rendering, exact-event discovery, sanitization, idempotency markers, and non-downgrading labels
Standalone notification workflow uses contents read and issues write without accepting Issue commands
V2.1 separates package identity from exact predevelopment and development-delivery runtime identities
Project AI dialogue policy defaults to required and remains separate from Adaptive Grill
Post-Gate stable Member release confirmation binds exact version, build_id, package, and commit
Validate and close commands inspect exact registered remote member refs instead of requiring local submission copies
Remote round inspection resolves the newest commit for each exact submission directory and verifies that it remains reachable from the registered branch head
Historical submissions remain valid after later assignments advance the same registered member branch
Dashboard task cards prioritize the current collecting round while preserving historical totals
validate-round, close-round, complete-round-review, validate-stage, and close-stage accept an explicit remote
Authoritative round closure preserves observed ref, observed head, submitted time, and human confirmation projection
Remote acceptance projection distinguishes pending, accepted, invalid, and legacy-not-required without changing Gate state
Dashboard displays accepted only after an exact reachable member receipt is observed
Human Gate review template exposes fixed common sections plus G1, G2, and G3 substantive sections
Gate review initialization selects only the current stage section and binds the current source commit
Deterministic validation enforces member coverage, artifact coverage, repository-relative links, citations, comparison identity, and complete content
prepare-gate binds the normalized document hash and source fingerprint; stale material is rejected before approval
The exact reviewed Markdown is copied beside gate-decision.yaml in the frozen baseline
Coordinator V2.1 package regression suite: 51 tests, zero failures and zero errors
Unified Member V2.1 discovery validates package-contained CLI and protocol paths and selects by package version
Project initialization binds Member 1.8.1 runtime identity to the unified Member 2.1 package and exact repository commit
Confirmed unified Member releases reject incomplete package identity while preserving legacy flat release records
Legacy flat Member manifests remain discoverable and path-escape candidates are rejected
Development coordination separates A-C evidence branches from D task-scoped code branches
Development submission confirmation, independent review, CI, PR, and main reachability remain separate authority chains
G4 and G5 content contracts preserve release, rollout, rollback, incident, and closure human authority
V2.0 development CLI task preview confirmation, self-review rejection, exact-commit review, main ancestry, G4, rollout, and G5 tests

known_boundaries: The coordinator must explicitly decide whether requirement analysis requires adaptive Grill before preview confirmation.
Adaptive Grill cannot be enabled for other assignment kinds or independence modes.
human_collaboration none only disables Grill; it never waives the separate submission confirmation.
The coordinator does not fabricate member dialogue, personal stance, or human-owner confirmation; it only authorizes modes and validates submitted evidence.
Local explicit-human-owner records bind fields and workflow but are not described as cryptographic identity proof.
Member submission confirmation authorizes only the individual contribution submission and has no G1-G3 Gate effect.
Pre-1.7.5 tasks remain legacy-not-required and cannot be retroactively signed.
Coordinator V1.7.6-issued assignments require ai-sop-member V1.7.5 or a compatible higher version.
GitHub Issues and mentions are reminder-only projections and never authorize or mutate SOP state.
Remote collection requires the registered Git remote and member refs to be fetchable; unavailable refs remain missing or invalid and are never converted into local evidence.
Historical validation accepts only a submission commit that is still reachable from the exact registered member ref; branch rewrites or unreachable commits remain invalid.
Git pull and notification delivery are not remotely auditable acceptance events; the coordinator projects only explicit Member V1.8.1 receipts.
Assignment acceptance has gate_effect none and cannot satisfy submission, round closure, or Gate approval.
The human Gate review pack is a read-only projection; source corrections must be made in authoritative artifacts and regenerated.
The pack improves human readability but does not add a Gate, approver, consensus rule, member obligation, or authority path.
A prepared pack becomes stale when its normalized text or bound source fingerprint changes and must be prepared again.
A-C/G1-G3 uses coordinator_cli.py V1.8.5 identity; D-E/G4-G5 uses development_cli.py V2.0.0 identity, and the two task contracts must not be mixed.

Package version and task runtime version are independent identities; A-C release confirmation retains both plus runtime_profile, package path, and release commit, while the current D-E 2.0 package provenance remains repository-lock-bound.
Coordinator packages older than V2.1 do not understand runtime_releases and must not be used as the active discovery runtime after legacy Member root packages are removed.
